headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative reaction on limited evidence [E3], while Anthropic landed mixed-to-neutral with one positive note and one strong criticism [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively on limited evidence, with one comment calling the company disorganized and questioning its ability to build products in its current state [E3].
Anthropic was split between praise for Claude’s reverse-engineering ability and criticism of Claude Code’s flawed assumptions, leaving the sentiment mixed-to-neutral [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: While most comments are focused on the issue that they found, I’m more intrigued by the fact that Claude was able to reverse engineer so well. Lowering the skills bar needed to reverse engineer at this level could have its own AI-related implications.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly praises Claude's reverse-engineering ability as impressive, while noting broader implications.

evidence.excerpt: Twice I’ve used Claude Code for something important and complex. Stunning initial speed and time savings, all given back eventually as it became apparent that some fatally flawed assumptions were baked into the code right from the beginning. The initial speed is exactly what the article describes, a Loss Disguised as a Win.

annotations.rationale: The commenter reports serious failures with Claude Code, calling the apparent productivity gains a 'Loss Disguised as a Win' because of fatally flawed code assumptions.

evidence.excerpt: In my experience building a couple ChatGPT apps and, through colleagues, working with OpenAI folks, I'm not sure they should be building anything in their current state. Seems quite disorganized over there.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes OpenAI as too disorganized to be building additional products, which is strong negative sentiment about the company's execution and strategy.
